source.task: Coordinate a shared Project Model contract across build-arena, elenchus-core, and arena-calibration.
source.issue: Project Model v0 parent coordination issue

goal: Keep three downstream implementation agents aligned on one pre-code decomposition contract before any repo adds incompatible F3 semantics.
nonGoals: Do not make Elenchus a truth oracle.
Do not require live paid model calls for acceptance.
Do not ask arena-calibration to invent the product decomposition it is supposed to test.

components.name: Contract authority
responsibilities: Publish versioned field semantics for downstream repos.
Name F3 as real load-bearing reasoning aimed at wrong target, component, sequence, or example.

components.name: Child ticket alignment
responsibilities: Ensure each child ticket names the same compatibility target and does not redefine decomposition.

components.name: Fixture feedback loop
responsibilities: Allow calibration fixture failures to update the parent contract without letting fixture code become the product owner.

dependencies.description: Child repos must target the parent Project Model v0 contract before adding local adapters or fixtures.
Child implementation gaps and fixture failures feed back into the parent contract.

invariants.description: Elenchus signals are advisory evidence, not objective truth or auto-approval.
Architecture, process, and strategy components still need observable checks.

observableChecks.description: Compare docs and schema for required field names and F3 definition markers.
observableChecks.observableSignal: local tests validate examples and scan docs for required contract terms

observableChecks.description: Reviewer checks non-code components have visible artifacts and evidence.
observableChecks.observableSignal: checklist marks each non-code component with artifact and pass/fail condition

observableChecks.description: Read child issue bodies and verify each names project-model/v0.
observableChecks.observableSignal: all child issues link to parent and same compatibility target

observableChecks.description: Verify ownership split is explicit and compatible.
observableChecks.observableSignal: no child issue asks elenchus-core or arena-calibration to redefine the Project Model

observableChecks.description: Record a contract gap as a verification gap or compatibility question.
observableChecks.observableSignal: parent issue/spec records failed fixture family and proposed adjustment

evidenceRequirements.description: GitHub issue body excerpts proving compatibility target and ownership split.
A checklist, ADR, tabletop transcript, or dry-run log that can be inspected.

assumptions.description: The build-arena parent issue is the source of truth for v0 rollout.
Each child repo can validate or map local types to the schema fields.

risks.description: Child agents may use local meanings for decomposition or observable check.
risks.mitigation: Require issue bodies and acceptance criteria to target project-model/v0 explicitly.

risks.description: A vague parent model would reward checklist matching instead of target alignment.
risks.mitigation: Run quality-gate checks for missing checks, dependencies, surfaces, and probes.

nearNeighborAlternatives.description: Let each repo define its own compatible-looking Project Model shape.
nearNeighborAlternatives.whyNotPrimary: The repos would drift on F3 and observable-check semantics.

nearNeighborAlternatives.description: Ask arena-calibration to create F3 fixtures before build-arena defines the model contract.
nearNeighborAlternatives.whyNotPrimary: Calibration fixtures need a stable ruler.

heldOutProbes.scenario: A child issue uses local names while claiming compatibility.
heldOutProbes.expectedBehavior: The compatibility check flags missing schemaVersion/project-model/v0 mapping.

heldOutProbes.scenario: An architecture component has no ADR, issue body, checklist, or artifact.
heldOutProbes.expectedBehavior: The quality gate flags no observable check or unclassified surface.
